The French legislative election of 1871, held in the aftermath of the collapse of the regime of Napoleon III, resulted in a monarchist majority in the French National Assembly that was favourable to making a peace agreement with Prussia. baseline.final_answer: In the late 1700s, French society was divided into three legal categories: the First Estate (the Catholic Church), the Second Estate (the nobility), and the Third Estate (the commoners).
